title: jatropha pohliana var. mollissima
intro: Jatropha pohliana var. mollissima, also known as the mollissima jatropha, is a variant of the Jatropha pohliana plant, a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes. The health benefits of Jatropha pohliana var. mollissima include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing the leaves, stems, or roots in hot water to create teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
